original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory `setReferralFee` missing bounds allows global swap DoS via underflow in Pair fee accrual
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If feeManager sets MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or a customReferralFees[pair] above 10000, Pair._update0/_update1 compute referralFee = amount * fee / 10000 which becomes > amount, and then `amount -= _referralerralFee` underflows and reverts, breaking swaps for all pairs using that fee setting.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete invariant breach already described in Phase A (INV-ACCESS-001). Even though it requires privileged access, it is an acute operational hazard and a common real-world outcome of compromised admin keys or misconfiguration.

proof_of_signal.lines: setReferralFee lacks require(_refFee<=10000); setCustomReferralFee lacks require(_refFee<=10000)
proof_of_signal.pattern: unbounded basis-point style parameter used in Pair arithmetic subtraction

preconditions: Attacker controls feeManager (or compromises it) OR governance misconfigures referral fees
At least one swap attempts to accrue fees via Pair._update0/_update1 (normal swap path)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: missing upper bound leading to arithmetic underflow revert
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: DoS of all swaps across all pairs

analysis_questions: Are there any other paths that use getReferralFee() and could revert unexpectedly?
Is referral fee applied to fee-amount or input amount consistently across stable/volatile?
Is there any emergency mechanism to reset referral fee if feeManager is compromised?
validation_requirements: Unit test: setReferralFee(10001) then attempt Pair.swap and observe revert at amount subtraction
Check if any contracts cache referral fee and could be bricked even after reset

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised feeManager sets MAX_REFERRAL_FEE=20000. Next user swap triggers _update0/_update1 and reverts due to underflow, halting swaps protocol-wide.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Protocol-wide trading halt (availability loss); potential cascading liquidations/market disruption elsewhere
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All traders and LPs across all pairs
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Possible if feeManager recovered; otherwise prolonged outage

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No config-bound tests for referral fee bounds and swap liveness

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
